[212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 左氏春秋 - Refers to the *Zuo Zhuan* (《左傳》), a classical commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals* (《春秋》).
  • 镌二卷 - A term for a two-volume edition or version, possibly indicating a critical or annotated edition.
  • 浙江巡抚采进 - Refers to the process by which the Zhejiang provincial governor collected and submitted the work to the imperial court.
  • 明陆粲撰 - Indicates that the work was written by Lu Can (陸粲), a scholar from the Ming dynasty.
  • 左传附注 - Refers to Lu Can's earlier work, *Zuo Zhuan Fu Zhu* (《左傳附注》), which is a commentary on the *Zuo Zhuan*.
  • 是编乃其由工科给事中坐劾张璁桂萼谪都匀驿丞时途中所作 - This phrase describes the circumstances under which Lu Can wrote the work. It means 'This edition was written by him when he was exiled to the position of postal station officer in Duyun after being impeached as a censor in the Ministry of Works for criticizing Zhang Cong and Gui E.'
  • 纠正左氏议论之失 - Means 'correcting the errors in the arguments of the *Zuo Zhuan*.'
  • 柳宗元非国语之类 - Refers to Liu Zongyuan's *Critique of Guoyu* (《非國語》), a work that critiques the *Guoyu* (《國語》), another classical text. The phrase means 'similar to Liu Zongyuan's critique of the *Guoyu*.'
  • 于左氏释经之谬辟之可也 - Means 'it is possible to correct the errors in the *Zuo Zhuan's* interpretation of the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.'
  • 记事记言但各从其实事乖言谬咎在古人与纪载者无与也 - This phrase is somewhat ambiguous but can be interpreted as 'recording events and words should follow the actual facts; if the facts are wrong or the words are erroneous, the blame lies with the ancient people and the recorders, not with the present writer.'
  • 谓之镌左则非其罪矣 - Means 'calling it a critique of the *Zuo Zhuan* would not be a fault.'
  • 甚哉其固也 - Means 'how stubborn it is,' referring to the *Zuo Zhuan* or possibly to Lu Can's approach.
  • 四库全书总目?经部?春秋类存目 - Refers to the *Siku Quanshu Zongmu* (《四庫全書總目》), the catalog of the *Siku Quanshu* (《四庫全書》), which is the largest collection of Chinese literature. The phrase indicates that this work is listed under the *Spring and Autumn* section of the *Siku Quanshu Zongmu*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 左氏春秋 - The *Zuo Zhuan* is one of the most important classical texts in Chinese history, a detailed commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 浙江巡抚 - The Zhejiang provincial governor, who played a role in the submission of this work to the imperial court.
  • 工科给事中 - A censor in the Ministry of Works, a position that Lu Can held before his impeachment.
  • 张璁桂萼 - Zhang Cong and Gui E were prominent officials during the Ming dynasty, and Lu Can criticized them, leading to his exile.
  • 都匀驿丞 - A low-ranking official position in Duyun, where Lu Can was exiled.
  • 柳宗元 - Liu Zongyuan was a famous Tang dynasty writer and philosopher known for his critical works, including *Critique of Guoyu*.
  • 国语 - The *Guoyu* is another classical text, a collection of state histories and speeches.
  • 四库全书总目 - The *Siku Quanshu Zongmu* is the catalog of the *Siku Quanshu*, a massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 左氏春秋 - The *Zuo Zhuan*.
  • 浙江巡抚 - The Zhejiang provincial governor.
  • 明陆粲 - Lu Can, the author of the work.
  • 张璁 - Zhang Cong, a Ming dynasty official.
  • 桂萼 - Gui E, a Ming dynasty official.
  • 都匀驿丞 - A low-ranking official position in Duyun.
  • 柳宗元 - Liu Zongyuan, a Tang dynasty writer and philosopher.
  • 国语 - The *Guoyu*.
  • 四库全书总目 - The *Siku Quanshu Zongmu*.

[213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兩淮鹽政采進本 - Refers to a collection compiled by the salt administration of the Two Huai regions, indicating the source of the text.
  • 詞館 - Refers to the Hanlin Academy, where scholars were stationed to draft imperial edicts and memorials.
  • 偃蹇 - Describes someone who is stubborn, arrogant, or out of favor, often used in the context of officials who are not promoted or are dismissed.
  • 息意邱園 - Means to withdraw from official life and retire to the countryside, often associated with a desire for a simpler, more peaceful life.
  • 研心經史 - Means to study the classics and history deeply, emphasizing scholarly dedication.
  • 授受 - Refers to the transmission of knowledge or teachings from one person to another.
  • 出入左氏 - Means to draw inspiration from or be influenced by the *Zuo Zhuan* (Commentary of Zuo), a classical Chinese text.
  • 專法馬班雄 - Means to specialize in the styles of Sima Qian and Ban Gu, two famous historians, and to emulate their powerful and majestic writing.
  • 東漢諸家所不及 - Means that the writing surpasses the works of all the writers of the Eastern Han period.
  • 推奬 - Means to praise or commend someone highly.
  • 平心之論 - Means an impartial or fair evaluation.
  • 無媿色 - Means to have no shame or regret, indicating that one can face criticism without feeling embarrassed.
  • 憶父詩 - Refers to a poem about one's father, often a personal and emotional piece.
  • 七歲所作 - Indicates that the poem was written when the author was seven years old.
  • 風格老成 - Means the style is mature and sophisticated, suggesting that the work is beyond the author's years.
  • 有香山新樂府遺音 - Refers to the influence of Bai Juyi's *New Music Bureau Poems* (新樂府), a collection of poems that often addressed social issues.
  • 贈別王直夫二首 - Refers to two farewell poems written for Wang Zhifu, a contemporary or friend of the author.
  • 篇什無多 - Means that the author has not written many poems, often used as a modest way to acknowledge a limited body of work.
  • 曾子固不能詩 - Refers to the idea that Ceng Ziqi (曾巩), a famous Song dynasty writer, was not particularly skilled at poetry, implying that the author's poetry is better than Ceng Ziqi's.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陸子余集 - The collected works of Lu Can, a Ming dynasty scholar and writer.
  • 明陸粲 - Lu Can, a Ming dynasty scholar and writer.
  • 張璁 - Zhang Cong, a prominent official during the Ming dynasty known for his political reforms.
  • 桂萼 - Gui E, another prominent official during the Ming dynasty, often mentioned alongside Zhang Cong.
  • 工科 - The Ministry of Works, one of the six ministries in the Ming government.
  • 劾 - To impeach or accuse someone of wrongdoing.
  • 王鏊 - Wang Ao, a famous Ming dynasty statesman and scholar, who was Lu Can's teacher.
  • 王文恪 - Wang Wenke, another name for Wang Ao, emphasizing his title as a respected scholar.
  • 徐時行 - Xu Shixing, a Ming dynasty scholar and friend of Lu Can.
  • 彭年 - Peng Nian, another Ming dynasty scholar and friend of Lu Can.
  • 左傳 - The *Zuo Zhuan*, a classical Chinese text that provides commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 司馬遷 - Sima Qian, a famous Han dynasty historian and author of the *Records of the Grand Historian*.
  • 馬班 - Sima Qian and Ban Gu, two famous historians whose works are highly regarded in Chinese literature.
  • 雄深雅健 - A term used to describe writing that is powerful, profound, elegant, and robust.
  • 東漢 - The Eastern Han dynasty, a period in Chinese history known for its literary achievements.
  • 香山 - Refers to Bai Juyi, a famous Tang dynasty poet known for his *New Music Bureau Poems*.
  • 曾子固 - Ceng Ziqi, a famous Song dynasty writer known for his prose but not his poetry.
  • 四庫全書總目 - The *General Catalogue of the Four Treasuries*, a massive bibliographic work compiled during the Qing dynasty.

[214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 附注 - To add annotations or notes alongside the text.
  • 音切 - Refers to phonetic analysis or pronunciation.
  • 私淑 - To admire and follow someone's teachings without being a direct disciple.
  • 镌 - To critique or comment on a text, often to clarify or correct it.
  • 辨疑 - To distinguish or resolve doubts and uncertainties.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陆粲 - A historical figure, likely a scholar or official during the Ming dynasty.
  • 左传 - The 'Zuo Zhuan,' one of the oldest and most important commentaries on the 'Spring and Autumn Annals.'
  • 注疏 - Annotations and explanations of classical texts.
  • 释文 - A commentary on the pronunciation and meaning of classical texts.
  • 左丘明 - Traditionally attributed as the author of the 'Zuo Zhuan,' though this is debated.
  • 战国 - The Warring States period, a time of political fragmentation and conflict in ancient China.
  • 孔子 - Confucius, the great philosopher and founder of Confucianism.
  • 嘉靖辛卯 - The year 1541 during the reign of Emperor Jiajing of the Ming dynasty.
  • 长洲 - A district in Suzhou, known for its cultural and scholarly activities.
  • 科给事中 - A high-ranking official in the Ming dynasty's imperial court.
  • 张桂 - A historical figure, possibly a political rival or opponent of Lu Can.
  • 都匀驿丞 - An official position in the postal service, likely a lower-ranking post.
  • 永新知县 - The position of magistrate in Yongxin county.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆粲 - Lu Can, a scholar and official.
  • 左传 - The 'Zuo Zhuan.'
  • 注疏 - Annotations and explanations.
  • 释文 - Commentary on pronunciation and meaning.
  • 左丘明 - Traditionally attributed author of the 'Zuo Zhuan.'
  • 战国 - The Warring States period.
  • 孔子 - Confucius.
  • 嘉靖辛卯 - The year 1541.
  • 长洲 - A district in Suzhou.
  • 科给事中 - A high-ranking official.
  • 张桂 - A historical figure.
  • 都匀驿丞 - An official position.
  • 永新知县 - The position of magistrate in Yongxin county.

[215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嘉靖丙戌 - Year 1536 during the reign of Emperor Jiajing of the Ming dynasty; '丙戌' is the sexagenary cycle notation.
  • 劲挺敢言 - Describes someone who is bold and outspoken, unafraid to speak their mind.
  • 擅政 - Means 'to wield power' or 'to dominate politics,' often with negative connotations.
  • 劾其罪 - Means 'to impeach for their crimes,' indicating a formal accusation.
  • 立斥之 - Means 'to immediately dismiss or expel them,' showing the swift action taken by the emperor.
  • 复用 - Means 'to reinstate' or 'to use again,' indicating a return to power.
  • 谪都镇驿丞 - Means 'demoted to the position of postal station manager in Duzhen,' a lower-ranking position.
  • 迁永新知县 - Means 'promoted to the position of magistrate in Yongxin,' indicating a move to a different administrative role.
  • 获盗数百 - Means 'to capture hundreds of bandits,' indicating significant success in law enforcement.
  • 奸猾屏迹 - Means 'the wicked and cunning were driven away,' indicating a reduction in criminal activity.
  • 乞归 - Means 'to request to return home,' often used when an official resigns or retires.
  • 毁甚 - Means 'suffered greatly from grief,' indicating a deep emotional response, often to the death of a loved one.
  • 未终丧而卒 - Means 'died before completing the mourning period,' indicating an untimely death.
  • 嗜学 - Means 'loved learning,' indicating a strong passion for education.
  • 博通古今 - Means 'broadly knowledgeable about ancient and modern times,' indicating a wide range of learning.
  • 著述行世 - Means 'wrote works that were published and circulated,' indicating the dissemination of his writings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉靖 - Era name of Emperor Jiajing (1521-1567), a significant period in the Ming dynasty.

[216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 直言忤旨 - Speaking frankly against the emperor's wishes.
  • 骨鲠臣 - Refers to a loyal and outspoken minister.
  • 流窜戮辱 - Being exiled, killed, or humiliated.
  • 气未尝折 - Never having been broken in spirit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉靖丙戌 - Refers to the year 1556 during the reign of Emperor Jiajing.
  • 庶吉士 - A prestigious position for young scholars in the imperial court.
  • 给事中 - A censor who could submit memorials to the emperor.
  • 驿丞 - A low-ranking official responsible for managing a post station.
  • 永新知县 - Refers to the position of magistrate in Yongxin.
  • 贞山稾 - Refers to a collection of writings by Lu Can.
  • 顾元言 - A commentator who provides additional context.
  • 李唐四杰 - Refers to the four outstanding writers of the Tang dynasty: Wang Bo, Yang Jiong, Lu Zhaolin, and Luo Binwang.
  • 张桂 - A person who was known for his hostility towards literati.
  • 都匀驿丞 - A postmaster in Duyun.
  • 菽园著述 - Refers to the works published by Lu Can.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆粲 - Lu Can, the subject of the passage.
  • 嘉靖 - The era name of Emperor Jiajing.
  • 庶吉士 - A prestigious position for young scholars.
  • 工科给事中 - A censor in the Ministry of Works.
  • 张桂 - A person who was hostile towards literati.
  • 都匀 - A place name, to be transliterated as 'Duyun'.
  • 永新 - A place name, to be transliterated as 'Yongxin'.
  • 贞山稾 - A collection of writings by Lu Can.
  • 顾元言 - A commentator.
  • 屠文升 - A contemporary scholar.
  • 袁永之 - A contemporary scholar.
  • 王汝化 - A contemporary scholar.
  • 李唐四杰 - The Four Great Writers of the Tang dynasty.
  • 贵溪分宜 - Refers to a place or person, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 菽园著述 - The works published by Lu Can.

[217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 前工科给事中 - Title meaning 'former censor of the Ministry of Works,' indicating a former official position.
  • 赠太常寺少卿 - Posthumous title meaning 'posthumously awarded the rank of Deputy Director of the Imperial Sacrifices Office,' indicating a posthumous honor.
  • 贞山陆公 - Respectful title meaning 'Mr. Lu of Zhen Mountain,' referring to the subject of the tombstone inscription.
  • 墓碑 - Literally 'tombstone,' but in this context, it refers to an epitaph or memorial inscription.
  • （明）王世贞撰 - Indicates that the text was written by Wang Shizhen of the Ming dynasty.
  • （四库全书?集部?别集类?弇州四部稿续稿卷一百三十四） - Bibliographic reference to the location of the text within the *Siku Quanshu* (Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature), specifying the collection, section, and volume.
  • 讳 - Respectful term used to indicate a person's name, often followed by the name itself.
  • 字 - Courtesy name, a secondary name given to a person upon reaching adulthood.
  • 一字 - Another courtesy name, used in addition to the primary one.
  • 其先 - Refers to the ancestors or forebears of the subject.
  • 豫章都尉 - Title meaning 'Commandant of Yuzhang,' indicating a military or administrative position.
  • 烈 - The name of the ancestor.
  • 吴令 - Title meaning 'Governor of Wu,' indicating a local administrative position.
  • 胥屏亭 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in Wu.
  • 长洲 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region or district.
  • 陈湖 - A place name, likely referring to a specific lake or area.
  • 诸生 - Refers to a student or scholar, specifically someone who has passed the county-level examination.
  • 皭然 - Term meaning 'purely' or 'clearly,' often used to describe someone who is morally upright.
  • 经术秇文 - Refers to scholarly work based on Confucian classics and literature.
  • 少傅 - Title meaning 'Vice Tutor,' indicating a high-ranking official position.
  • 王文恪公 - Posthumous title meaning 'Posthumously awarded the rank of Wenzhike,' indicating a posthumous honor.
  • 举于应天 - Refers to passing the provincial examination in the capital city of Nanjing.
  • 会试 - Refers to the metropolitan examination, a higher-level examination held in the capital.
  • 春秋 - Refers to the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, a classic text.
  • 故辅 - Refers to a former chancellor or high-ranking official.
  • 石文介公珤 - Posthumous title meaning 'Posthumously awarded the rank of Shiwenzhike,' indicating a posthumous honor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞山 - Place name, likely referring to a specific mountain or area.
  • 陆公 - Refers to the subject of the tombstone inscription, Mr. Lu.
  • 王世贞 - Famous Ming dynasty scholar and writer, known for his literary works and critical writings.
  • 四库全书 - The *Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature*, a massive compilation of Chinese texts commissioned during the Qing dynasty.
  • 集部 - One of the four main categories of the *Siku Quanshu*, focusing on collections of poetry, prose, and other literary works.
  • 别集类 - Subcategory of *集部* that includes individual collections of works by specific authors.
  • 弇州四部稿续稿 - Collection of Wang Shizhen's works, specifically the second part of his *Four Departments Collection*.
  • 豫章 - Ancient name for the Jiangxi province, specifically the area around Nanchang.
  • 吴 - Ancient state in the Jiangsu province, often used to refer to the region around Suzhou and Wuxi.
  • 长洲 - Ancient name for the area around Suzhou.
  • 陈湖 - Specific lake or area in the region.
  • 太常寺 - Imperial Sacrifices Office, responsible for religious and ceremonial matters.
  • 少卿 - Rank within the Imperial Sacrifices Office, equivalent to a deputy director.
  • 经术 - Study of Confucian classics and their interpretation.
  • 秇文 - Scholarly writing or essays.
  • 少傅 - High-ranking official position, often serving as a tutor to the emperor.
  • 王文恪公 - Posthumous title given to a high-ranking official, indicating his moral and intellectual achievements.
  • 应天 - Capital city of the Ming dynasty, now known as Nanjing.
  • 会试 - Metropolitan examination, a crucial step in the imperial examination system.
  • 春秋 - The *Spring and Autumn Annals*, a classic text that records the history of the state of Lu during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 故辅 - Refers to a former chancellor or high-ranking official, likely a significant figure in the political and cultural landscape of the time.
  • 石文介公珤 - Posthumous title given to a high-ranking official, indicating his moral and intellectual achievements.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆公 - Mr. Lu, the subject of the tombstone inscription.
  • 王世贞 - The author of the tombstone inscription.
  • 贞山 - Place name, likely referring to a specific mountain or area.
  • 豫章 - Ancient name for the Jiangxi province, specifically the area around Nanchang.
  • 吴 - Ancient state in the Jiangsu province, often used to refer to the region around Suzhou and Wuxi.
  • 长洲 - Ancient name for the area around Suzhou.
  • 陈湖 - Specific lake or area in the region.
  • 太常寺 - Imperial Sacrifices Office.
  • 少卿 - Rank within the Imperial Sacrifices Office.
  • 经术 - Study of Confucian classics and their interpretation.
  • 秇文 - Scholarly writing or essays.
  • 少傅 - High-ranking official position, often serving as a tutor to the emperor.
  • 王文恪公 - Posthumous title given to a high-ranking official.
  • 应天 - Capital city of the Ming dynasty, now known as Nanjing.
  • 会试 - Metropolitan examination.
  • 春秋 - The *Spring and Autumn Annals*.
  • 故辅 - Former chancellor or high-ranking official.
  • 石文介公珤 - Posthumous title given to a high-ranking official.

[218]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大竒之拔寘第一 - Indicates that Dai Qi was placed first in a certain ranking or selection process.
  • 公鄉中有忌者 - Indicates that there were people in the same hometown as Gong who were jealous of him.
  • 匿公策他所 - Means 'hid Gong's strategy somewhere else.'
  • 俟且半而后出之 - Means 'waited until almost halfway through the process before revealing it.'
  • 石公大恨 - Means 'Shi Gong was greatly resentful.'
  • 吾为人所卖 - Means 'I was betrayed by someone.'
  • 公闻之亦不肯名石公客 - Means 'Gong heard about it and would not call Shi Gong a guest.'
  • 以选改翰林庶吉士 - Means 'changed to be selected as a Hanlin Scholar.'
  • 凡七试皆居第一 - Means 'in all seven examinations, he ranked first.'
  • 经生慕称之 - Means 'scholars admired and praised him.'
  • 王文恪公 - Refers to a posthumous name or honorific title given to a scholar-official.
  • 伯仲庐陵眉山 - Refers to two famous scholars or writers, likely Ouyang Xiu (庐陵) and Su Shi (眉山).
  • 其谈说古文辞者 - Means 'those who discuss and write ancient literary works.'
  • 学成当授官 - Means 'after completing his studies, he was to be appointed to an official position.'
  • 首辅杨文襄公 - Refers to a high-ranking official, likely Yang Tinghe (杨廷和), a prominent statesman of the Ming dynasty.
  • 试之复拟第一 - Means 'tested him again and proposed him as first.'
  • 留充史馆议礼诸臣 - Means 'kept him to serve in the History Office as an advisor on rituals.'
  • 骤贵而张桂尤幸用事 - Means 'became suddenly wealthy and powerful, with Zhang Gui being particularly favored and wielding influence.'
  • 各为密疏 - Means 'each wrote secret memorials.'
  • 诸吉士皆前相宏私人也 - Means 'all the scholars were former associates of Premier Hong.'
  • 上惑之 - Means 'the emperor was deceived by this.'
  • 俾各出就选人 - Means 'ordered them to step down from their positions.'
  • 公拜给事中之三日 - Means 'On the third day after Gong took office as Censor.'
  • 即上疏言京边诸要务 - Means 'he immediately submitted a memorial discussing important matters concerning the capital and border regions.'
  • 杨公读而叹曰 - Means 'Yang Gong read it and sighed, saying:'
  • 吾向者以文士待陆生几失之 - Means 'I treated Lu Gong as just a literary scholar, nearly missing out on his true potential.'
  • 贾长沙之俦 - Refers to Jia Yi, a famous literary figure and political advisor of the Han dynasty.
  • 生家敬舆未论 - This phrase is ambiguous but could mean 'his family's respect for him is not discussed' or 'his family's standing is not mentioned.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大竒 - Refers to Dai Qi, likely a historical figure.
  • 石公 - Refers to Shi Gong, likely another historical figure.
  • 杨文襄公 - Refers to Yang Tinghe, a prominent statesman of the Ming dynasty.
  • 张桂 - Refers to Zhang Gui, likely a historical figure.
  • 王文恪公 - Refers to a posthumous name or honorific title, possibly for a scholar-official.
  • 庐陵眉山 - Refers to Ouyang Xiu (庐陵) and Su Shi (眉山), two famous scholars and writers of the Song dynasty.
  • 贾长沙 - Refers to Jia Yi, a famous literary figure and political advisor of the Han dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 大竒 - Dai Qi
  • 石公 - Shi Gong
  • 杨文襄公 - Yang Tinghe
  • 张桂 - Zhang Gui
  • 王文恪公 - Wang Wenke Gong (likely a posthumous name or honorific title)
  • 庐陵 - Ouyang Xiu
  • 眉山 - Su Shi
  • 贾长沙 - Jia Yi

[219]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 也公益感奮 - 益感奮 means 'increasingly moved and inspired,' indicating a growing emotional response.
  • 時政四事 - 時政 refers to current political affairs, and 四事 means 'four matters' or 'four issues.'
  • 不易言者 - 不易言 means 'difficult to express' or 'hard to articulate,' suggesting that these issues were sensitive or controversial.
  • 月中疏凡三上 - 月中 means 'during the month,' and 疏凡三上 means 'submitted three memorials in total.'
  • 楊公愈欲一當公 - 一當公 means 'to take charge' or 'to assume responsibility,' indicating that Yang wanted to handle the matter personally.
  • 公愈自引避 - 自引避 means 'to withdraw oneself voluntarily,' suggesting that the person avoided taking responsibility.
  • 中貴人不便之 - 中貴人 refers to eunuchs or high-ranking officials, and 不便之 means 'found it inconvenient' or 'objected to it.'
  • 蜚语中公 - 蜚语 means 'slander' or 'false rumors,' and 中公 means 'targeted at him,' indicating that false accusations were directed at the person.
  • 公发其奸 - 发其奸 means 'exposed his deceit' or 'uncovered his corruption.'
  • 条列牧政十事 - 条列 means 'to list' or 'to enumerate,' and 牧政 refers to administrative policies or governance measures.
  • 报可 - 报可 means 'approved' or 'accepted,' indicating that the suggestions were accepted.
  • 诸阉气夺 - 诸阉 refers to the eunuchs, and 气夺 means 'their morale was broken' or 'they were intimidated.'
  • 用京朝官主各省试 - 京朝官 refers to officials from the capital, and 主各省试 means 'to oversee the provincial examinations.'
  • 其程义雅驯 - 程义 refers to the content of the exam papers, and 雅驯 means 'elegant and refined,' indicating that the papers were of high quality.
  • 天下冠 - 天下冠 means 'the best in the world' or 'the most outstanding,' suggesting that the papers were exemplary.
  • 守故职 - 故职 means 'former position' or 'previous role,' indicating that the person returned to their old job.
